name: CORE Assessment
description: Evaluates foundational thinking skills for AI-assisted work environments. Measures pattern recognition, problem decomposition, critical observation, logical reasoning, and output validation.

sections.name: Rapid Pattern Recognition
sections.description: Instinctive logical reasoning and pattern detection under time pressure.

sections.name: Problem Decomposition
sections.description: Breaking complex problems into logical, sequenced steps with correct dependencies.

sections.name: Critical Observation
sections.description: Spotting ambiguity, missing requirements, unstated assumptions, and contradictions.

sections.name: Logical Reasoning Under Constraints
sections.description: Deductive and conditional reasoning, constraint handling, multi-step logic chains.

sections.name: Output Validation
sections.description: Evaluating whether outputs satisfy original intent — not just whether they look right.

classifications.label: Exceptional
classifications.description: Strong instincts across all dimensions. Likely to excel in AI-directed work with minimal guidance.

classifications.label: Proficient
classifications.description: Solid reasoning and observation skills. May need development in 1–2 areas but fundamentally capable.

classifications.label: Developing
classifications.description: Shows potential in some areas but has gaps that would require structured coaching.

classifications.label: Foundational
classifications.description: Basic logical reasoning present but critical gaps in decomposition, observation, or validation.

classifications.label: Needs Significant Development
classifications.description: Fundamental reasoning skills need building before AI-assisted work is viable.

single_select.description: Full points if correct, 0 if incorrect.

anomaly_flags.suspicious_fast_description: Response submitted in under the question's suspicious_fast_seconds threshold — may indicate random selection
anomaly_flags.slow_threshold_description: Response consumed nearly all available time — may indicate overthinking or struggling
